request.question: CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will increase doxorubicin-induced cytotoxicity measured by LDH release

hypothesis.readiness_rationale: Core fields are populated, but comparator and success criterion are not specified.
clarifying_questions: What is the experimental control group for the comparison?
What is the success criterion for increased cytotoxicity?
What is the mechanistic rationale behind the predicted increase in cytotoxicity?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: CRISPR TP53 knockout iPSC cardiomyocytes doxorubicin LDH release
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will increase doxorubicin-induced cytotoxicity measured by LDH release

references.title: Cellular and electrophysiological characterization of triadin knockout syndrome using induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cardiomyocytes
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses a CRISPR knockout in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, showcasing methodology and context relevant to the proposed study, although it focuses on a different gene.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of CRISPR knockout of TP53 is a specific application of gene editing, but while a related paper (3) discusses CRISPR in the context of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, it focuses on a different gene (TRDN) and its implications on cardiac arrhythmia. However, it connects with the biological system and uses a similar experimental approach. Thus, while not directly testing TP53's knockout in cardiomyocytes, the core experimental setup aligns with the intended study. No papers demonstrated the exact intervention being tested (TP53), hence we classify the work as similar rather than exact match found.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To determine the cytotoxicity of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes following CRISPR knockout of TP53.
plan.experimental_design: The experiment involves generating TP53 knockout iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes using CRISPR technology, followed by a comparative analysis with a control group of wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes. The cytotoxicity will be assessed through lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) release measurements over a defined period.
controls: Treatment with 1% Triton X-100 to induce complete cell lysis, serving as a positive control for LDH release.
Untreated wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes to establish baseline LDH release levels.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 2-3 days | Cell Culture Preparation
Expand human induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) culture in Essential 8 Flex medium at 37°C with 5% CO2 until they reach 80% confluency in T-175 flasks (2-3 days).
Expected outcome: iPSCs reach 80% confluency in T-175 flasks.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 20 min | Transfection Reagents Setup
Prepare CRISPR/Cas9 ribonucleoprotein (RNP) complex by mixing 100 µg of Cas9 protein (Nusystems) and 100 µg of TP53 sgRNA (Synthego) in 300 µL of Opti-MEM medium for 20 min at room temperature.
Expected outcome: RNP complex prepared and ready for transfection.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Transfection
Transfect 1 × 10^6 iPSCs with 300 µL of the CRISPR/Cas9 RNP complex using the Neon electroporator with settings: 1300 V, 20 ms, 2 pulses.
Expected outcome: Successful transfection of iPSCs, evidenced by cellular viability post-transfection.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Recovery
Culture transfected iPSCs in Essential 8 Flex medium at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 48 h before selection.
Expected outcome: iPSCs exhibit recovery post-transfection.

DAY 1 | 72 h | Cell Selection
Use 10 µg/mL puromycin to select for successfully transfected iPSCs for 72 h at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Enrichment of TP53 knockout iPSCs.

DAY 3 | 48 h | Differentiation to Cardiomyocytes
Initiate differentiation of selected iPSCs into cardiomyocytes using RPMI 1640 medium supplemented with B27 (without insulin) and 10 µM CHIR99021 for 48 h at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Differentiation of iPSCs into cardiomyocytes.

DAY 5 | 5 days | Cardiomyocyte Maturation
Replace medium with RPMI 1640 + B27 (with insulin) and culture for additional 5 days at 37°C with 5% CO2 for maturation.
Expected outcome: Mature iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes prepared.

DAY 10 | 24 h | Cytotoxicity Assay Setup
Plate 2 × 10^4 iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes per well in 96-well plates and allow to adhere for 24 h at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Cardiomyocytes adhere and spread on the plate.

DAY 11 | 2 h | LDH Release Assay - Positive Control
Add 100 µL of 1% Triton X-100 to triplicate wells for positive control for 2 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: LDH release from positive control wells increases significantly.

DAY 11 | 24 h | LDH Release Assay - Knockout
Add test solutions (50 µL of increasing concentrations of doxorubicin from 0 to 10 µM) to wells containing TP53 knockout cardiomyocytes for 24 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: LDH release measured over a range of doxorubicin concentrations.

DAY 12 | 24 h | LDH Release Assay - Control
Add 50 µL of vehicle control (DMSO) to wells containing wild-type cardiomyocytes for 24 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Baseline LDH release from wild-type cardiomyocytes is established.

DAY 13 | 1 h | LDH Measurement
Evaluate LDH activity in culture supernatants using LDH cytotoxicity assay kit according to manufacturer instructions. Measure absorbance at 490 nm using a microplate reader.
Expected outcome: LDH activity quantitated in relation to control.

DAY 13 | 30 min | Data Collection
Record the absorbance values from the microplate reader for all experimental groups.
Expected outcome: Complete dataset of absorbance values across all conditions.

DAY 14 | 1 h | Statistical Analysis
Conduct statistical analysis using one-way ANOVA followed by Tukey's post-hoc test to compare LDH release between groups with a significance threshold of p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined for LDH release among groups.

DAY 14 | 1 h | Results Interpretation
Interpret results in context of TP53 knockout effect on cardiomyocyte cytotoxicity by comparing mean absorbance values from experimental and control groups.
Expected outcome: Clear conclusion regarding the cytotoxicity bias attributable to TP53 knockout.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: CRISPR technology effectively knocks out TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes.
iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes differentiate successfully into functional cardiomyocytes.
LDH release is a reliable marker for cytotoxicity in cardiomyocytes.
decision_criteria: Proceed if LDH release from TP53 knockout cardiomyocytes is statistically significantly elevated compared to wild-type.
Validate if cytotoxic threshold for doxorubicin treatment is reached (assay dependent).
Ensure positive control results meet expected LDH release levels (e.g., >2000 U/L for Triton X-100).
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same iPSC line for all experiments to ensure consistency.
Standardize medium conditions and supplements used across all cultures.
Ensure calibration of the microplate reader is regularly performed before measurements.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: (success criterion TBD) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the cytotoxicity of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes following CRISPR knockout of TP53.

Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
